title: Repeater Delay Line
description: A chain of repeaters to delay a signal. Each repeater adds 1–4 redstone ticks (0.1–0.4 s). Chain them for longer delays. Also boosts signal strength back to 15 after every repeater — essential for long wire runs. Click a repeater to change its delay.

steps.title: Stone base
steps.explanation: Place a line of stone blocks as a base. Each stone will hold one repeater.

steps.title: Input lever
steps.explanation: Place a lever at the west end to drive the signal. Flip it to send a pulse through all the repeaters.

steps.title: Repeater chain
steps.explanation: Place 3 repeaters on the remaining stones, all facing east (delay=1 each). Signal travels left to right with a 1-tick delay per repeater. Click a repeater to increase its delay — the lit indicator shows the delay tick count.

steps.title: Output lamp
steps.explanation: Add a lamp at the far end. It lights after the combined delay of all repeaters. Total delay here = 3 ticks (0.15 s). Add more repeaters or increase delays to extend the gap.
